proposed_from.topic: Sharp partial identification of the average treatment effect on the treated (ATET) in staggered-adoption panel data under a total-variation (TV) bound on the pre-trend discrepancy — replacing the sup-norm / Lipschitz smoothness relaxations of Rambachan & Roth (2023). Establish a closed-form sharp width in terms of the TV-discrepancy bound and the pre-treatment outcome dispersion, characterise the critical-TV-discrepancy frontier separating sign-identification from vacuity, and exhibit when the closed form strictly tightens the sup-norm bound.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-repo objects are `q1_partial_did_unobserved_trends / p1_baseline_v2`, which names sharp partial identification for staggered DiD with partially unobserved pretrends, and `panel_honest_did_phase / v1`, which asks whether aggregate sensitivity equals the cohort-level support-function image. Neither names the focal object used here: a joint anchored-TV support body for a finite family of weighted post-treatment ATT aggregates with scalar frontiers as projections. The downgraded `pid_synth_control_factor_misspec / v1` and `pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec / v1` are methodological neighbors because they exploit signed paths and joint support geometry, but they live in different substantive clusters and do not duplicate the staggered-DiD kernel.","published_axis":"The closest published paper is RambachanRoth2023CrediblePT, which supplies scalar Honest-DiD sensitivity classes, especially relative-magnitude and smoothness restrictions, but not a joint TV body for several ATT aggregates under one shared post-treatment discrepancy path. CallawaySantAnna2021MultiplePeriods and BorusyakJaravelSpiess2024EventStudies provide the family-of-estimands and aggregation maps, yet neither paper studies partial identification under a common TV sensitivity class. ManskiPepper2018BoundedVariation justifies variation restrictions abstractly, but not the family support-body geometry or the strict-comparator theorem against coordinatewise relative-magnitude rectangles. The epsilon-gap is therefore not “TV instead of sup norm” in one dimension; it is the move from separate scalar envelopes to one multi-target identified set whose support function is closed form and whose shape can be strictly non-rectangular."}
seed_list: Derive the sharp anchored-TV ATET interval, the computable dual norm Gamma_TV(omega), and the critical TV radii for sign loss and vacuity; prove strict narrowing versus the Rambachan-Roth relative-magnitude box when Gamma_TV(omega) < ||omega||_1.

Characterize exactly when an aggregate event-time TV restriction is the projection of the cohort-level staggered-adoption TV sensitivity set, with strict conservative and anti-conservative witness designs.
Derive separate analytic frontiers for zero-entry and full vacuity of the TV-restricted ATET set under cohort trimming, and show when each frontier jumps.
Construct TV-calibrated ATET intervals that remain valid after pretrend-test rejection by using the observed pretrend path directly rather than conditioning on test passage.
Show that the same anchored TV support norm jointly identifies a family of weighted post-treatment ATT aggregates, not only one scalar ATET.
Give necessary and sufficient conditions on the signed bias weights and the observed pretrend path for the TV interval to be a strict subset of the Rambachan-Roth relative-magnitude interval.
Promote the strict-gap witness from one hand-tuned path to an open class of observable laws with oscillating bias weights, proving generic TV improvement over rectangular envelopes.
Characterize the extreme paths of the anchored TV sensitivity class and show that ATET sharpness reduces to a support-function over monotone-hinge extensions.
Show that cohort-level cancellation can make aggregate Honest-DiD TV sensitivity anti-conservative even when the aggregate pretrend path is identically zero.
Layer sampling uncertainty onto the anchored TV identified set without conditioning on passed pretrend diagnostics, yielding a valid breakdown-value confidence band.

seed_details.one_line: Derive the sharp anchored-TV ATET interval, the computable dual norm Gamma_TV(omega), and the critical TV radii for sign loss and vacuity; prove strict narrowing versus the Rambachan-Roth relative-magnitude box when Gamma_TV(omega) < ||omega||_1.

anchor_paper.author: Rambachan and Roth
anchor_paper.claim: Honest DiD allows researcher-chosen restriction classes but does not supply the TV-specific closed-form ATET support norm targeted here.
anchor_paper.location: Introduction, p. 2557 in the published version (same language appears on p. 1 of the preprint)
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: we place restrictions on the possible values of the post-treatment difference in trends

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens/replaces an assumption class; stronger because the surprise is a computable dual norm and strict interval shrinkage, not just a broader class.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from RambachanRoth2023CrediblePT because TV is just another polyhedral Delta and the support function is standard.
seed_details.rebuttal: RR allow arbitrary Delta, but they do not derive the ATET-specific TV dual norm, the analytic sign/vacuity frontiers, or the strict-comparison condition Gamma_TV(omega) < ||omega||_1.

adversarial_referee.instance: Headline names frontiers, but without a computable object it is only generic sensitivity.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebutted by naming Gamma_TV(omega) and using it directly in width, sign-loss, and vacuity formulas.

adversarial_referee.instance: Strict tightening may be only a sufficient condition, not a real frontier.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Handled by separating Theorem 2's sufficient strictness test from Conjecture 3's sharper projection frontier.

adversarial_referee.instance: RR already permit arbitrary restriction sets, so a TV class may be only a user-input variant.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: False at the statement level: the proposal's contribution is the named TV support norm and closed-form ATET frontier, not the permission to choose Delta.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize exactly when an aggregate event-time TV restriction is the projection of the cohort-level staggered-adoption TV sensitivity set, with strict conservative and anti-conservative witness designs.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith archive
anchor_paper.claim: The open quotient-support obstacle is already flagged in the banked Honest-DiD phase proposal.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends a support-function warning from one sensitivity geometry to TV; stronger because it predicts both conservative and anti-conservative projection failure.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from SunAbraham2021DynamicEffects because aggregation problems in staggered DiD are already known.
seed_details.rebuttal: Sun-Abraham diagnose coefficient contamination, not identified-set projection of a cohort-level TV ball; the mathematical object is different.

adversarial_referee.instance: The necessity side of the projection criterion could be missing.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: This is why the statement is kept as a Conjecture rather than upgraded to a Theorem.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could collapse to a one-sided conservatism claim rather than a full iff frontier.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The seed explicitly demands strict conservative and anti-conservative witness designs, not only one direction.

adversarial_referee.instance: Cancellation examples could be backward-engineered and non-generic.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The intended statement promotes the witness to an operator condition plus open-class neighborhoods around the active face.

seed_details.one_line: Derive separate analytic frontiers for zero-entry and full vacuity of the TV-restricted ATET set under cohort trimming, and show when each frontier jumps.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith archive
anchor_paper.claim: Prior work in the bank already flagged discontinuity and moving-critical-value issues but not a TV-specific theorem.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime-shift seed; stronger because it targets distinct sign-loss and vacuity thresholds and possible jumps under trimming.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just a numerical reporting refinement of RR-style breakdown values.
seed_details.rebuttal: The novelty is the analytic separation of the first zero-entry radius from the full-vacuity radius, with possible discontinuities induced by staggered trimming.

adversarial_referee.instance: Breakdown values are mentioned, but the focal object may not be computable.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The seed names two explicit scalars, kappa_sign and kappa_vac, as the focal objects.

adversarial_referee.instance: RothSantAnnaBilinskiPoe2023 already recommend stating what violations overturn conclusions.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Recommendation is not an analytic frontier theorem, especially not one with trimming-driven jumps.

adversarial_referee.instance: Jumps could depend on knife-edge ties only.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The seed survives only if the jump witness promotes beyond a single tie set; otherwise it should be downgraded later.

seed_details.one_line: Construct TV-calibrated ATET intervals that remain valid after pretrend-test rejection by using the observed pretrend path directly rather than conditioning on test passage.

anchor_paper.author: Roth
anchor_paper.claim: Pretrend testing has low power and conditioning on passing can distort estimation and confidence intervals.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Sensitivity-with-consumer seed; weaker unless it delivers a new sharp identified set rather than just advice against pretesting.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just rephrasing Roth2022PretestCaution with a different robustness parameter.
seed_details.rebuttal: The kernel would be a population identified set under a TV restriction, not a warning about pretest-based conditional inference.

seed_details.one_line: Show that the same anchored TV support norm jointly identifies a family of weighted post-treatment ATT aggregates, not only one scalar ATET.

anchor_paper.author: Callaway and Sant'Anna
anchor_paper.claim: Group-time effects and aggregation schemes are identified under conditional parallel trends.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Joint-family seed; stronger if the same support object genuinely identifies several named aggregates from one primitive class.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is only a routine restatement from one scalar ATT to several weighted averages.
seed_details.rebuttal: It is worth keeping only if one support norm simultaneously controls multiple named ATT aggregates; otherwise it should be dropped later.

seed_details.one_line: Give necessary and sufficient conditions on the signed bias weights and the observed pretrend path for the TV interval to be a strict subset of the Rambachan-Roth relative-magnitude interval.

anchor_paper.author: Rambachan and Roth
anchor_paper.claim: Relative-magnitude restrictions compare post-treatment violations to the maximal pre-treatment violation.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Strict-subset theorem; stronger if it proves an iff frontier rather than only another sufficient shrinking condition.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from norm inequality comparisons between TV and sup-norm balls.
seed_details.rebuttal: The nontrivial part is the same-target ATET interval comparison after passing through signed staggered bias weights and the observed pretrend calibration.

seed_details.one_line: Promote the strict-gap witness from one hand-tuned path to an open class of observable laws with oscillating bias weights, proving generic TV improvement over rectangular envelopes.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith archive
anchor_paper.claim: Prior in-repo work already used the lesson that joint support functions can be strictly tighter than rectangular envelopes.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: strictly narrower and kinked differently from the rectangular outer envelope

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample-search seed; stronger only if promoted to an open class rather than a single witness.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just a recycled open-set witness template from another partial-ID problem.
seed_details.rebuttal: The template is old, but the new content would be the ATET TV geometry and the staggered bias-weight condition defining the open class.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize the extreme paths of the anchored TV sensitivity class and show that ATET sharpness reduces to a support-function over monotone-hinge extensions.

anchor_paper.author: Manski and Pepper
anchor_paper.claim: Bounded-variation restrictions can discipline ambiguous policy effects without point-identifying assumptions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: New-object seed; stronger if the extreme-path characterization yields a genuine evaluable object, weaker if it is just LP language in disguise.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a definitional unpacking of variation balls and support functions.
seed_details.rebuttal: It survives only if the extreme paths are explicit enough to evaluate the ATET width without solving a generic optimization every time.

seed_details.one_line: Show that cohort-level cancellation can make aggregate Honest-DiD TV sensitivity anti-conservative even when the aggregate pretrend path is identically zero.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith archive
anchor_paper.claim: The aggregate-versus-cohort gap was already isolated as a live open issue in the Honest-DiD bank.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample seed; stronger because it targets a named published workflow and a nondegenerate cancellation geometry rather than a self-invented shortcut.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just another contamination example like SunAbraham2021DynamicEffects.
seed_details.rebuttal: The object here is anti-conservative partial identification after aggregation, not treatment-effect contamination of a point estimator.

seed_details.one_line: Layer sampling uncertainty onto the anchored TV identified set without conditioning on passed pretrend diagnostics, yielding a valid breakdown-value confidence band.

anchor_paper.author: Roth
anchor_paper.claim: Conditioning on pretrend-test passage can distort estimates and intervals.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Estimation-duality seed; weaker unless the identification object is already sharp and the inference layer is genuinely non-conditional.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is an inference wrapper, not a new identification theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: That objection lands unless the identification kernel is solved first, so this seed stays below flagship for cold-start selection.

proposed_from.literature_map:
1. Manski and Pepper (2018, RESTAT, ManskiPepper2018BoundedVariation) anchor bounded-variation partial identification under ambiguous policy effects.
2. Kahn-Lang and Lang (2020, JBES, KahnLangLang2020PromisePitfalls) argue that pretrends are informative but not dispositive.
3. Roth (2022, AER: Insights, Roth2022PretestCaution) shows low power and pretest-selection distortions in pretrend testing.
4. Rambachan and Roth (2023, ReStud, RambachanRoth2023CrediblePT) cast DiD sensitivity as partial identification under researcher-chosen violation classes.
5. Roth, Sant'Anna, Bilinski, and Poe (2023, JoE, RothSantAnnaBilinskiPoe2023TrendingDiD) push sensitivity analysis as standard practice when exact parallel trends is doubtful.
6. Callaway and Sant'Anna (2021, JoE, CallawaySantAnna2021MultiplePeriods) identify group-time ATT objects and aggregation schemes in staggered adoption.
7. Sun and Abraham (2021, JoE, SunAbraham2021DynamicEffects) show staggered event-study contamination under heterogeneity.
8. Goodman-Bacon (2021, JoE, GoodmanBacon2021Timing) and de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille (2020, AER) diagnose weighting pathologies in staggered DiD.
9. Borusyak, Jaravel, and Spiess (2024, ReStud, BorusyakJaravelSpiess2024EventStudies) clarify efficient imputation-based event-study aggregation.
10. The unresolved niche is a TV-based, closed-form, sharp ATET width/frontier that uses the signed pretrend path and separates sign loss from full vacuity.
11. A second unresolved niche is whether aggregate event-time sensitivity classes are sharp projections of cohort-level staggered-adoption classes.
12. Prior CausalSmith proposals already flagged support-function projection and strict-envelope issues, but not this TV-specific kernel.

Manski and Pepper (2018, RESTAT) anchor bounded-variation partial identification for DiD-style policy ambiguity. 2. Freyaldenhoven, Hansen, and Shapiro (2019, AER) handle pre-event trends using proxy covariates for latent confounds. 3. Kahn-Lang and Lang (2020, JBES) stress that parallel pretrends are neither necessary nor sufficient for counterfactual parallel trends. 4. Roth (2022, AER Insights) shows low power and pretest bias in event-study pretrend testing. 5. Bilinski and Hatfield (2026, Statistics in Medicine) develop non-inferiority/equivalence tests for meaningful pretrend violations. 6. Rambachan and Roth (2023, RESTUD) give Honest DiD partial identification under RM/sup-norm and smoothness restrictions. 7. Roth, Sant'Anna, Bilinski, and Poe (2023, Journal of Econometrics) synthesize DiD advances and motivate sensitivity analysis beyond diagnostics. 8. Callaway and Sant'Anna (2021, Journal of Econometrics) define group-time ATT estimands for staggered adoption under conditional parallel trends. 9. Sun and Abraham (2021, Journal of Econometrics) show TWFE event-study contamination and false apparent pretrends under heterogeneity. 10. de Chaisemartin and D'Haultfoeuille (2020, AER) and Goodman-Bacon (2021, Journal of Econometrics) decompose TWFE weighting pathologies. 11. Borusyak, Jaravel, and Spiess (2024, RESTUD) provide robust efficient imputation for staggered event studies under parallel trends. 12. Ding and Li (2019, Political Analysis) and Ye, Keele, Hasegawa, and Small (2024, JASA) develop bracketing-style partial identification alternatives. 13. Roth and Sant'Anna (2023, Econometrica) show parallel trends can be sensitive to outcome functional form, suggesting scale-aware sensitivity choices. 14. Neither names the focal object used here: a joint anchored-TV support body for a finite family of weighted post-treatment ATT aggregates with scalar frontiers as projections. The downgraded `pid_synth_control_factor_misspec / v1` and `pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec / v1` are methodological neighbors because they exploit signed paths and joint support geometry, but they live in different substantive clusters and do not duplicate the staggered-DiD kernel.","published_axis":"The closest published paper is RambachanRoth2023CrediblePT, which supplies scalar Honest-DiD sensitivity classes, especially relative-magnitude and smoothness restrictions, but not a joint TV body for several ATT aggregates under one shared post-treatment discrepancy path. CallawaySantAnna2021MultiplePeriods and BorusyakJaravelSpiess2024EventStudies provide the family-of-estimands and aggregation maps, yet neither paper studies partial identification under a common TV sensitivity class. ManskiPepper2018BoundedVariation justifies variation restrictions abstractly, but not the family support-body geometry or the strict-comparator theorem against coordinatewise relative-magnitude rectangles. The epsilon-gap is therefore not “TV instead of sup norm” in one dimension; it is the move from separate scalar envelopes to one multi-target identified set whose support function is closed form and whose shape can be strictly non-rectangular."},"literature_checklist":[{"author":"Ashesh Rambachan and Jonathan Roth","year":2023,"venue":"Review of Economic Studies","bibkey":"RambachanRoth2023CrediblePT","claim":"Honest DiD formulates sensitivity to trend violations under analyst-chosen restriction classes, with relative-magnitude and smoothness as leading examples.","relevant_to":"Theorem 1, Theorem 2"},{"author":"Charles F.
